programme.title: Planet A
programme.desc: Reforms to phase out the most polluting gases are increasing prices for certified imports, and fueling a black market for cheaper, illegal imports from China. But not all gases are illegal , so tracing illicit imports is difficult. Sanctions are low, profits are high. The global warming potential of one 10kg cylinder is the same as driving 1.5 times around the world.

programme.title: Eco Africa
programme.desc: Tackling plastic pollution in urban Nigeria, staying cool in Cairo, making city tourism more sustainable and using dance to inspire climate action.

programme.title: DocFilm
programme.desc: Invasive fungal infections are increasingly leading to life-threatening diseases worldwide. And in agriculture, fungi pose a massive threat to harvests. And the available antidotes for humans and plants are increasingly losing their effectiveness.

programme.title: Made in Germany
programme.desc: A premature foal survives thanks to an AI camera detecting a critical emergency. The same technology spots colic in horses and lameness in cows — reshaping modern livestock care.

programme.title: Planet A
programme.desc: Reforms to phase out the most polluting gases are increasing prices for certified imports, and fueling a black market for cheaper, illegal imports from China. But not all gases are illegal , so tracing illicit imports is difficult. Sanctions are low, profits are high. The global warming potential of one 10kg cylinder is the same as driving 1.5 times around the world.

programme.title: In Good Shape
programme.desc: Diet breads for weight loss, magic exercises for strong muscles, secret tricks for skin and hair: Many tips on the web are ineffective, some are dangerous, but others are promising. In Good Shape has put some trends under a microscope.

programme.title: Close up
programme.desc: A win for wildlife, a loss for humans. African nature reserves are restricting indigenous peoples' access to rivers and forests — and, in doing so, are endangering their very existence.

programme.title: Shift
programme.desc: Scientists are using AI in many environmental projects to help analyze data and accelerate processes. But at the same time, complex AI systems require significant resources. AI and sustainability: Is it a good fit?

programme.title: The 77 Percent
programme.desc: From the waves of Robertsport to the roads of East Africa, this episode crosses borders. We ask why it's so hard to move within Africa, follow a truck driver on the Northern Corridor, and discover Seychelles' Creole culture.

programme.title: Eco India
programme.desc: Retrofitting conventional vehicles could help Delhi go electric more equitably. Also: women farmers battle age-old structures — and making power from bird poop.

programme.title: DocFilm
programme.desc: Three young women from China are struggling to realize their dreams. While Chinese society is undergoing profound change, they are simultaneously feeling the social pressure to start a family and have children.

programme.title: Close up
programme.desc: A win for wildlife, a loss for humans. African nature reserves are restricting indigenous peoples' access to rivers and forests — and, in doing so, are endangering their very existence.

programme.title: Shift
programme.desc: Scientists are using AI in many environmental projects to help analyze data and accelerate processes. But at the same time, complex AI systems require significant resources. AI and sustainability: Is it a good fit?

programme.title: DocFilm
programme.desc: Rod fishing has a long tradition in the Azores. It is the only method of tuna fishing permitted on the Atlantic islands - and the most sustainable. A counterpoint to industrial fishing and "floating tuna factories.".

programme.title: Made in Germany
programme.desc: A premature foal survives thanks to an AI camera detecting a critical emergency. The same technology spots colic in horses and lameness in cows — reshaping modern livestock care.

programme.title: DocFilm
programme.desc: India's widows suffer from exclusion and stigmatization. For decades, they have been fighting for more rights, with the help of NGOs. For the first time, widows are allowed to celebrate the festival of colors at the Gopinath Temple - a breakthrough.

programme.title: Mapped Out
programme.desc: Japan is shifting from passive defense to active deterrence vis a vis China over Taiwan. It's a gamble to assert itself in the Indo-Pacific and to reshape its relationship with the US, its longtime security guarantor.

programme.title: Eco Africa
programme.desc: Tackling plastic pollution in urban Nigeria, staying cool in Cairo, making city tourism more sustainable and using dance to inspire climate action.

programme.title: DocFilm
programme.desc: Three young women from China are struggling to realize their dreams. While Chinese society is undergoing profound change, they are simultaneously feeling the social pressure to start a family and have children.

programme.title: Eco India
programme.desc: Retrofitting conventional vehicles could help Delhi go electric more equitably. Also: women farmers battle age-old structures — and making power from bird poop.

programme.title: Made in Germany
programme.desc: A premature foal survives thanks to an AI camera detecting a critical emergency. The same technology spots colic in horses and lameness in cows — reshaping modern livestock care.

programme.title: Mapped Out
programme.desc: Japan is shifting from passive defense to active deterrence vis a vis China over Taiwan. It's a gamble to assert itself in the Indo-Pacific and to reshape its relationship with the US, its longtime security guarantor.

programme.title: Close up
programme.desc: A win for wildlife, a loss for humans. African nature reserves are restricting indigenous peoples' access to rivers and forests — and, in doing so, are endangering their very existence.

programme.title: DocFilm
programme.desc: Rod fishing has a long tradition in the Azores. It is the only method of tuna fishing permitted on the Atlantic islands - and the most sustainable. A counterpoint to industrial fishing and "floating tuna factories.".

programme.title: Mapped Out
programme.desc: Japan is shifting from passive defense to active deterrence vis a vis China over Taiwan. It's a gamble to assert itself in the Indo-Pacific and to reshape its relationship with the US, its longtime security guarantor.

programme.title: The 77 Percent
programme.desc: From the waves of Robertsport to the roads of East Africa, this episode crosses borders. We ask why it's so hard to move within Africa, follow a truck driver on the Northern Corridor, and discover Seychelles' Creole culture.

programme.title: DocFilm
programme.desc: Three young women from China are struggling to realize their dreams. While Chinese society is undergoing profound change, they are simultaneously feeling the social pressure to start a family and have children.

programme.title: Made in Germany
programme.desc: A premature foal survives thanks to an AI camera detecting a critical emergency. The same technology spots colic in horses and lameness in cows — reshaping modern livestock care.

programme.title: Mapped Out
programme.desc: Japan is shifting from passive defense to active deterrence vis a vis China over Taiwan. It's a gamble to assert itself in the Indo-Pacific and to reshape its relationship with the US, its longtime security guarantor.

programme.title: Focus on Europe
programme.desc: No rule of law, no free media, Orban loyalists everywhere — how quickly can change come? +++ The Baltic states fear a war with Russia.

programme.title: Arts Unveiled
programme.desc: The 1980s may seem like the distant past. But some of the decade's historical and pop cultural events were so significant that their effects are still felt today. We go beyond nostalgia to reckon with their legacies.

programme.title: DocFilm
programme.desc: Liberland is a country that doesn't officially exist. A country that promises voluntary taxes and a lean state. It's a libertarian utopia that's finding more supporters worldwide.

programme.title: Made in Germany
programme.desc: A premature foal survives thanks to an AI camera detecting a critical emergency. The same technology spots colic in horses and lameness in cows — reshaping modern livestock care.

programme.title: Mapped Out
programme.desc: Japan is shifting from passive defense to active deterrence vis a vis China over Taiwan. It's a gamble to assert itself in the Indo-Pacific and to reshape its relationship with the US, its longtime security guarantor.

programme.title: Eco India
programme.desc: Retrofitting conventional vehicles could help Delhi go electric more equitably. Also: women farmers battle age-old structures — and making power from bird poop.

programme.title: DocFilm
programme.desc: Liberland is a country that doesn't officially exist. A country that promises voluntary taxes and a lean state. It's a libertarian utopia that's finding more supporters worldwide.

programme.title: Focus on Europe
programme.desc: No rule of law, no free media, Orban loyalists everywhere — how quickly can change come? +++ The Baltic states fear a war with Russia.

programme.title: Mapped Out
programme.desc: Japan is shifting from passive defense to active deterrence vis a vis China over Taiwan. It's a gamble to assert itself in the Indo-Pacific and to reshape its relationship with the US, its longtime security guarantor.

programme.title: DocFilm
programme.desc: Rod fishing has a long tradition in the Azores. It is the only method of tuna fishing permitted on the Atlantic islands - and the most sustainable. A counterpoint to industrial fishing and "floating tuna factories.".

programme.title: DocFilm
programme.desc: Three young women from China are struggling to realize their dreams. While Chinese society is undergoing profound change, they are simultaneously feeling the social pressure to start a family and have children.

programme.title: Made in Germany
programme.desc: A premature foal survives thanks to an AI camera detecting a critical emergency. The same technology spots colic in horses and lameness in cows — reshaping modern livestock care.

programme.title: Focus on Europe
programme.desc: No rule of law, no free media, Orban loyalists everywhere — how quickly can change come? +++ The Baltic states fear a war with Russia.

programme.title: DocFilm
programme.desc: Invasive fungal infections are increasingly leading to life-threatening diseases worldwide. And in agriculture, fungi pose a massive threat to harvests. And the available antidotes for humans and plants are increasingly losing their effectiveness.

programme.title: DocFilm
programme.desc: Liberland is a country that doesn't officially exist. A country that promises voluntary taxes and a lean state. It's a libertarian utopia that's finding more supporters worldwide.

programme.title: Sports Life
programme.desc: 23-year-old Luisa Rizzo feels most at home when piloting a drone - either as a cinematographer, or as one of the foremost pilots in the fast-growing sport of drone racing. Being born with spinal muscular atrophy means Luisa spends much of her time in a wheelchair, and racing has given her a sense of freedom and purpose. Can she continue her sporting success at the highly competitive World Cup?

programme.title: Focus on Europe
programme.desc: No rule of law, no free media, Orban loyalists everywhere — how quickly can change come? +++ The Baltic states fear a war with Russia.

programme.title: DocFilm
programme.desc: Three young women from China are struggling to realize their dreams. While Chinese society is undergoing profound change, they are simultaneously feeling the social pressure to start a family and have children.

programme.title: Business Beyond
programme.desc: Low-cost carrier IndiGo dominates Indian skies. But when revised pilot rest rules took effect in late 2025, planning failures left nearly a million passengers stranded, exposing risks in a near-monopoly aviation market.

programme.title: REV
programme.desc: Malta's hand-painted buses, each one unique, once ferried locals and tourists alike. Now few remain, lovingly restored by specialist coachbuilders. We also ride with police on speed patrol and test the VinFast VF 6.

programme.title: DocFilm
programme.desc: Invasive fungal infections are increasingly leading to life-threatening diseases worldwide. And in agriculture, fungi pose a massive threat to harvests. And the available antidotes for humans and plants are increasingly losing their effectiveness.

programme.title: In Good Shape
programme.desc: Diet breads for weight loss, magic exercises for strong muscles, secret tricks for skin and hair: Many tips on the web are ineffective, some are dangerous, but others are promising. In Good Shape has put some trends under a microscope.

programme.title: Focus on Europe
programme.desc: No rule of law, no free media, Orban loyalists everywhere — how quickly can change come? +++ The Baltic states fear a war with Russia.

programme.title: Business Beyond
programme.desc: Low-cost carrier IndiGo dominates Indian skies. But when revised pilot rest rules took effect in late 2025, planning failures left nearly a million passengers stranded, exposing risks in a near-monopoly aviation market.

programme.title: Business Beyond
programme.desc: Low-cost carrier IndiGo dominates Indian skies. But when revised pilot rest rules took effect in late 2025, planning failures left nearly a million passengers stranded, exposing risks in a near-monopoly aviation market.

programme.title: DocFilm
programme.desc: Invasive fungal infections are increasingly leading to life-threatening diseases worldwide. And in agriculture, fungi pose a massive threat to harvests. And the available antidotes for humans and plants are increasingly losing their effectiveness.

programme.title: Business Beyond
programme.desc: Low-cost carrier IndiGo dominates Indian skies. But when revised pilot rest rules took effect in late 2025, planning failures left nearly a million passengers stranded, exposing risks in a near-monopoly aviation market.

programme.title: Best of DW Podcasts
programme.desc: Select DW podcasts enrich the program with current analyses of world politics and its influence on our daily lives. There will also be space in the program for lively discussions about social standards and taboos.

programme.title: DocFilm
programme.desc: With the Russian invasion of Ukraine, fears of a nuclear accident have returned. In the very first days of the war, Russian troops occupied the exclusion zone around what's left of Chernobyl. Shortly afterwards, they took control of Zaporizhzhia's nuclear power plant.

programme.title: Focus on Europe
programme.desc: No rule of law, no free media, Orban loyalists everywhere — how quickly can change come? +++ The Baltic states fear a war with Russia.
